siteMeta.description: Practical injection technique, reconstitution math, calculators, and supply guides for people prescribed injectable medications. Patient-education only.

hero.subtitle: A wayfinding atlas for peptide injection — sites, technique, rotation, and the troubleshooting of a hundred small things that go wrong.
hero.meta: Patient-education only · No vendor links · Reviewed against the package insert
hero.h1: Injections, done right — without the guesswork.
hero.dek: Step-numbered technique guides, reconstitution math, and site-rotation maps — written by an RN, reviewed by an MD, and cited to nursing-education literature you can verify yourself.

home.cited_to_literature: Cited to nursing-education literature & package inserts
home.library_dek: Eight foundational guides, from drawing up a dose to rotating sites without scarring.
home.most_read_heading: What people are reading this week.
home.by_topic_heading: Browse by what you are doing.
home.how_we_work_heading: Two independent sources before a sentence ships.
home.how_we_work_body: Every guide on InjectCompass is written from manufacturer Instructions for Use, CDC and WHO injection-safety guidance, and the nursing-education literature. Two independent sources must agree before a claim ships, and a credentialed reviewer signs the article once editorial review is complete.
home.stop_heading: When this site is the wrong source.
home.stop_body: Nothing on InjectCompass is a substitute for the prescription your clinician wrote, the IFU that came with your medication, or the judgment of the healthcare professional who knows your case. If a symptom does not match what you read here, treat what you read as out of date and call your prescriber.

atlas.indexSubhead: Listed in the order a clinician would teach the material. Page numbers are stable across editions.
atlas.atlasNotShelf: An atlas, not an article shelf.
atlas.atlasNotShelfBody: Each entry in this site is a numbered chapter, grouped into one of four parts. You can read it cover to cover, or open the index and jump to the page you need.

categories.injection_sites_dek: Subcutaneous and intramuscular sites — anatomy, landmarks, and what to feel for.
categories.technique_dek: Drawing up, pinching, angle of entry, and the small motor patterns that make injections painless.
categories.rotation_dek: Grids, calendars, and the rotation logic that prevents lipohypertrophy and scar tissue.
categories.troubleshooting_dek: Bruising, leakage, cloudy solutions, and the dozen other things that go wrong on real days.
categories.drugs_compounds_dek: Generic and brand names, package-insert summaries, and the safety notes the leaflet leaves out.
categories.tools_dek: Reconstitution math, syringe converters, and a printable dose-schedule builder.

trust.independent_review: Independent peer review · No vendor sponsorship · No telehealth deals

article.injection_sites_in_this_article: Injection sites discussed in this article
article.translation_pending_body: The chrome of this page is in your language; the article body below remains in English while we complete the translation.

newsletter_inline.title: Get evidence-based injection guidance
newsletter_inline.description: Once a week. No vendors, no upsells.

methodology.label: Methodology · Edition v1.2
methodology.title: How a chapter gets written.
methodology.body: We work from the manufacturer Instructions for Use, the relevant CDC and WHO injection-safety guidance, and the nursing-education literature. Two independent sources have to agree before a chapter is published.

pipeline.label: Pipeline · 8 in research
pipeline.title: The chapters we are working on.
pipeline.body: The pipeline is public. Every chapter we are researching is listed with its working title, the questions it tries to answer, and the sources we have read so far.

stop.title: Stop · seek prescriber
stop.body: Nothing on InjectCompass is a substitute for the instructions that came with your medication or for advice from the clinician who prescribed it.

footer.patientEducationOnly: Educational and patient-education purposes only. We do not link to research-peptide vendors. We do not accept payment from drug manufacturers or telehealth clinics.
footer.disclaimer_body: InjectCompass publishes patient-education content. Nothing here is a substitute for the prescription your clinician wrote, the Instructions for Use that came with your medication, or the judgment of the healthcare professional who knows your case. If a symptom does not match what you read here, treat what you read as out of date and call your prescriber.
footer.copyright: © {year} InjectCompass. Patient-education only. Not medical advice.
